post.description: One-click login as the demo user. Only available when DEMO_MODE=true.

Clear the session cookie and invalidate the current JWT server-side.

Safe to call without a valid session — we still clear the cookie. When a
token is present (even expired), bump ``session_version`` so stolen cookies
cannot be reused after logout.

get.description:
Return the caller's own profile.

Intentionally skips the approval gate: a pending user must be able to
learn their own status so the frontend can show the "awaiting approval"
page instead of a bare 403. All data routes still require approval.
Help debug passkey 500s: checks webauthn import, config, and DB. Only available when WEBAUTHN_DEBUG=true.

post.description: Return WebAuthn options for creating a new account with a passkey.
Verify passkey registration and create user + credential. Body: { credential: <JSON from navigator.credentials.create> }.
Return WebAuthn options for signing in with a passkey.
Verify passkey assertion and return token. Body: { credential: <JSON from navigator.credentials.get> }.

get.description: List passkey credentials for the current user (id and created_at only).

schema.title: Response List Passkey Credentials Api Auth Passkey Credentials Get

delete.description: Remove a passkey credential. id is the row UUID (WebAuthnCredential.id).

post.description: Return WebAuthn registration options for adding a passkey to the current user (authenticated).
Verify passkey registration and add credential to current user. Returns 200 with { ok: true }.

get.description: Redirect the user to Google's OAuth consent screen.
Exchange code for tokens, get user info, create/update user, redirect to frontend with JWT.

post.description:
Exchange the one-time login code (HttpOnly cookie set by /google/callback) for a JWT.

The code used to ride on the /auth/callback URL query — that leaks into
browser history, Referer headers, and proxy access logs. Reading it from
a path-scoped, HttpOnly, short-TTL cookie keeps it out of all three.

On success, ``_token_response`` ALSO sets the session cookie (HttpOnly,
SameSite=Strict) so the user is logged in immediately — same as every
other login path (password / passkey / demo).

Response List Category Groups Api Categories Groups Get
Response Category Usage Api Categories Usage Get
Response Get Splits Api Transactions  Transaction Id  Splits Get
Response List Rules Api Rules Get

get.description: Heuristic recurring candidates from budget-account outflows (not yet saved as recurring).

schema.title: Response List Recurring Suggestions Api Recurring Suggestions Get
Response List Recurring Api Recurring Get

post.description: Return uncategorized transactions and categories for on-device LLM.

schema.title: Response List Goals Api Goals Get

get.description: Return all credit/loan accounts with their current balances.

schema.title: Response List Debt Accounts Api Debt Accounts Get

post.description:
Calculate a debt payoff plan using avalanche, snowball, or hybrid ordering.

Avalanche: pay off highest-interest debt first (minimises total interest).
Snowball: pay off lowest-balance debt first (psychological momentum).
Hybrid: highest APR first; ties (same APR) use smaller balance first. Optional
``priority_account_ids`` (debt account UUIDs) overrides initial ordering; any
missing ids are appended using the hybrid tie-break sort.
Return FSA scan candidates without calling a model (for on-device review).

patch.description: Update the claim/dismiss status of an FSA-reviewed transaction.

get.description: List all FSA review items for the household.

post.description:
Execute a parsed action intent (create transaction or debt account).

Gated on a single-use confirmation token — without it this would be an open
mutation endpoint accepting arbitrary payloads.
Validate a proposed action and issue a single-use confirmation token.

get.description: Budgeted vs actual per category for the current month (deterministic).
Savings-goal progress facts for the household (deterministic).
Structured financial snapshot (typed numbers/ids) for the verifier.
Category spending trends vs 3-month average (deterministic).
Deterministically flagged unusual expense transactions (model-free).
Per-account debt facts (deterministic) for rate suggestions.
Question-aware category/payee matches with SQL-computed sums (deterministic).
Return whether SimpleFIN is configured for this household.

post.description:
Claim a SimpleFIN setup token immediately and return discovered accounts.

Decodes the base64 token, POSTs to the claim URL, saves the access URL,
and fetches the account list for preview.

get.description: Return AI settings for this household.

put.description: Update AI settings for this household.

get.description: Return the household's saved debt plan preferences.

put.description: Update the household's saved debt plan preferences.
Record pay-cycle review signals; they reset when the resolved cycle start changes.

get.description: Look up curated cancellation steps by payee / merchant name (best-effort fuzzy match).

schema.title: Response List Cycle Commitments Api Cycle Commitments Get

post.description: Stream a Tier 4 (opt-in cloud) completion. Requires per-feature consent.

schema.title: Response List Consent Api Llm Consent Get

get.description:
Stream a JSON document containing every row tied to this user.

The document is assembled key-by-key. Heavy tables (transactions,
account_snapshots, llm_audit) use ``db.stream`` to avoid loading
everything into process memory before the first byte ships.

delete.description:
Hard-delete this user. Cascade-deletes the household if empty after.

Steps:
    1. Revoke any active LLM consent (defensive — race with retention
       or cache code that might re-check consent during cleanup).
    2. Best-effort cache purge.
    3. Delete llm_audit rows for this user (no FK; explicit delete).
    4. Delete llm_consent for this user (FK does CASCADE, but be
       explicit — protects us if the SQLite test backend skips FK
       enforcement).
    5. Delete webauthn_credentials for this user (CASCADE in DDL, but
       same defensive rationale).
    6. Delete the user row.
    7. If the household has no remaining users, delete the household
       and ALL tables that reference it. Most FKs are NO ACTION at the
       DB level (default), so we do explicit deletes in dependency
       order — see ``_delete_household_cascade``.
    8. Commit.

schema.title: Response Delete Me Api Me Delete

get.description:
Infrastructure health (Fly machines/regions) — admin-only: machine
names and regions are deployment fingerprints regular members don't need.

post.description:
Issue a magic link if the email maps to an existing user.

ALWAYS returns 200 with ``{"ok": true}``. The response shape is
identical whether or not the email exists in the DB. Combined with
the existing per-IP rate limit on /api/auth/*, this denies the
"enumerate which emails have accounts" oracle to any probing client.

Redeem the token, set the session cookie, return JSON.

POST with the token in the body (not the query string) so the token never
lands in server access logs. The frontend page calls this via fetch and
renders success/failure UI.
Returns:
    200 {"ok": true} + Set-Cookie session=...  on success
    400 {"detail": "..."} on invalid/expired/used token

get.description:
Return all users, newest first. Optional status filter for the
common "show me pending users" admin workflow.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status. Omit to return all users.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status. Omit to return all users.

schema.title: Response List Users Api Admin Users Get

post.description:
Flip the user's status to ``approved``. The next login attempt
(any path) will succeed instead of 403'ing.

Flip the user's status to ``rejected``. They keep their account row
but can never log in until an admin re-approves. Useful to deny without
permanently deleting (audit trail + appeal path).

get.description:
Public runtime config the browser needs before login.

Returns server-authoritative values (demo_mode, which auth methods are
configured) so the frontend doesn't have to rely on build-time
NEXT_PUBLIC_* vars — which can silently drift from the real backend
state (e.g. build with DEMO_MODE=false, redeploy backend with demo on).

Pre-auth by design: everything here is already inferrable from the
login page's visible affordances, so there's nothing to protect.

AdminUserItem.title: AdminUserItem
AdminUserItem.description:
Schema for an admin listing of a user. Includes status and role so the
admin UI can render filtering + role badges.

CancelGuideResponse.title: CancelGuideResponse
CancelGuideResponse.description: Always HTTP 200: matched=false uses generic_steps only.

ContextFacts.title: ContextFacts
ContextFacts.description:
Deterministic structured financial snapshot for the on-device verifier.

Typed numbers/ids only (no free-text blob). ``budget``/``goals`` reuse the
A1/A2 fact helpers so all fact endpoints share one source of truth.

CycleReviewUpdate.title: CycleReviewUpdate
CycleReviewUpdate.description: Partial update — only fields the client sends are applied.

confirm.title: Confirm
confirm.description: Must equal exactly: "delete my account and all data"

include_all_outflows.title: Include All Outflows
include_all_outflows.description: Send all scanned outflows to the LLM (max 500 rows). Higher cost/latency than keyword pre-filter.

HostingHealthResponse.title: HostingHealthResponse
HostingHealthResponse.description:
Top-level response. ``drift`` is empty when everything matches
the free-tier blueprint; non-empty list = render an amber warning.

``available`` at the top level is true if at least one app's data
came through; false only when the token is missing or both apps
errored. The card renders a graceful "unavailable" state in that
case rather than erroring.

MagicLinkRequestResponse.title: MagicLinkRequestResponse
MagicLinkRequestResponse.description:
Always returned regardless of whether the email exists.

Privacy: if a probing attacker enumerated "does this email exist on
Clarity?" by trying both /register and /magic-link/request, the
/register flow already returns 400 on duplicates. So the only place
enumeration could leak today IS this route. ``ok=True`` always.

PasskeyAuthenticateVerifyRequest.title: PasskeyAuthenticateVerifyRequest
PasskeyAuthenticateVerifyRequest.description: Body for POST /auth/passkey/authenticate/verify. credential = JSON from navigator.credentials.get().

PasskeyCredentialListItem.title: PasskeyCredentialListItem
PasskeyCredentialListItem.description: One passkey credential for list response (id and created_at only; no credential_id or public_key).

PasskeyRegisterVerifyRequest.title: PasskeyRegisterVerifyRequest
PasskeyRegisterVerifyRequest.description: Body for POST /auth/passkey/register/verify. credential = JSON from navigator.credentials.create().

SuggestCategoriesBody.title: SuggestCategoriesBody
SuggestCategoriesBody.description: Optional filters (same semantics as GET /transactions). Empty body = recent uncategorized (up to 50).

TokenResponse.title: TokenResponse
TokenResponse.description: Login response. Session is established via httpOnly cookie; access_token is omitted for browsers.
